data.title: Jury Says Tesla Must Pay $243 Million Over Fatal Autopilot Crash — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph De Avila

A Florida jury found Tesla was partly to blame over a fatal 2019 collision involving a vehicle equipped with the company's driver-assistance software, awarding the plaintiffs nearly $329 million in damages.

It marks the first time a jury has awarded damages in a lawsuit related to Tesla's driver-assistance features and a major setback for Elon Musk's electric-vehicle company. The jury concluded the automaker failed to provide sufficient warnings or instructions for its Autopilot feature in the 2019 Tesla Model S involved in the accident, making the car unreasonably dangerous.

"Tesla designed autopilot only for controlled access highways yet deliberately chose not to restrict drivers from using it elsewhere, alongside Elon Musk telling the world Autopilot drove better than humans, " said Brett Schreiber, an attorney for the plaintiffs. "Tesla's lies turned our roads into test tracks for their fundamentally flawed technology, putting everyday Americans like Naibel Benavides and Dillon Angulo in harm's way."

The case stems from a car crash in Key Largo, Fla., where the driver of a Tesla Model S, George McGee, hit a parked car that rammed into two pedestrians. The crash killed Naibel Benavides Leon and severely injured Dillon Angulo.

The renewed scrutiny of Tesla's Autopilot feature poses significant risk for the automaker, which has tied the company's future to self-driving cars and a new robotaxi business. Tesla is also confronting a sales slump stemming in part to owner Musk's foray into politics and his association with President Trump.

Benavides's family and Angulo sued Tesla, arguing the automaker's Autopilot technology had dangerous defects, which should have been fixed. Tesla said McGee, who had dropped his cellphone and was attempting to pick it up off the floor of the car when the collision occurred, was at fault.

The jury found that Tesla was 33% at fault for the crash and McGee was responsible for the remainder.

The jury awarded the plaintiffs $129 million in compensatory damages and $200 million in punitive damages. Tesla will have to pay a third of the compensatory damages and the entirety of the punitive damages for a total of $243 million in damages.

The Benavides family previously sued McGee and settled with him.

U.S. District Judge Beth Bloom in June dismissed some of the claims filled by the plaintiffs alleging defective manufacturing and negligent misrepresentation. Those claims centered on whether Tesla falsely advertised what its driver-assistance technology is capable of.

In California, regulators are seeking to suspend or terminate Tesla's licenses to sell vehicles in the state for at least 30 days, alleging the automaker has deceived consumers by falsely implying its cars could drive on their own. Tesla has denied the allegations in that case. An administrative judge is expected to rule on that case in the coming weeks.

Tesla has mostly avoided civil trials involving its driver-assistance technology. It settled a lawsuit in 2024 that involved a crash in 2018. The automaker settled additional lawsuits in 2024 and 2025.

A California jury in 2023 found there wasn't a manufacturing defect in the advanced driver-assistance system of a Tesla that veered off an interstate and hit a tree outside of Los Angeles in 2019.

In June, Tesla launched a small robotaxi service in Austin deploying a fleet of Model Y sport-utility vehicles operated by an advanced version of its Full Self-Driving software. Each car has a Tesla safety driver in the front passenger seat in case of emergency.

Tesla executives say they are looking to expand its robotaxi fleet to the San Francisco Bay Area, and are looking at Arizona, Florida and Nevada.

Write to Joseph De Avila at joseph.deavila@wsj.com

data.title: Promotora de Hoteles Norte 19 Announces Early Termination of Management Agreements for Three Hotels in Mexico
data.description:
Mexico City, August 1st, 2025  – Promotora de Hoteles Norte 19, S.A.B. de C.V.  (“Norte 19” or the “Company”)  announced the early termination of the management agreements for three hotels in its portfolio. This decision reflects the property owner’s strategy to integrate these assets into a unified family-operated business model.

Effective today, the following hotels will no longer be operated by the Company:

  • City Express by Marriott Torreón (115 rooms)
  • City Express by Marriott Monterrey Lindavista (130 rooms)
  • City Express by Marriott Monterrey Norte (115 rooms)

Following these adjustments, Norte 19’s portfolio will consist of 154 hotels and 17,932 rooms.

Miranda Newswire – For Full Press Release:  Go to Website

Investor Relations Contact Details

Héctor Vázquez

Corporate Finance and Investor Relations

Phone: +52 55-5249-8067

E-mail:  hvazquez@norte19.com

Ana Lucía SorsMiranda IRPhone: +52 833-293-4232E-mail:  ana.sors@miranda- partners.com 

 

About Norte 19 

Norte 19 is a Mexican company that offers integrated solutions for the hospitality industry, dedicated to providing services ranging from the design and development of hotels to the operation and management of first-class hospitality businesses. With a track record spanning more than two decades, Norte 19 has stood out for its commitment to excellence in service, innovation in technology, and efficiency in operation. Norte 19 operates more than 150 hotels in Mexico, Colombia, Costa Rica, and Chile, establishing strong partnerships with recognized brands and continuously adapting to market demands. Norte 19 will maintain the same ticker symbol (“HCITY”) on the Mexican Stock Exchange (BMV).

For further information, please visit our website:  https://norte19.com/en/investors

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1451 ET - Fair Isaac Corp. is still looking to raise prices on its mortgage credit-score metric despite rising competition. Mortgage brokers are now allowed to use the competing metric VantageScore to measure applicants' creditworthiness, thanks to a new federal rule. FICO CEO William Lansing tells analysts there is a gap between the value of the score and its pricing, which has increased eight-fold in the past five years. "We're always looking at how we're going to close that gap," he says. Lansing says FICO can manage the new competition because it already competes with VantageScore in non-mortgage lending. "We continue to be committed to that philosophy on price change," he says. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1128 ET — Apple is starting to spend on AI but it's way behind its tech competitors. Apple has so far invested $10 billion on AI, compared with $130 billion, $120 billion and $85 billion at Microsoft, Amazon and Google respectively, Quilter Cheviot analyst Ben Barringer says. Apple said during its earnings call Thursday it would substantially increase capital expenditures in the year ahead. While it's unlikely to match its competitors, increasing spending would be a positive sign that Apple is preparing to be more aggressive about its future capabilities, Barringer says. The company also hasn't ruled out mergers and acquisitions, which could help it catch up, Barringer adds. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1121 ET - Amazon didn't explicitly say AWS could accelerate in 3Q but analysts at BofA Securities think it can. The internet retailer's cloud-computing arm reported sales climbed 17.5%, which is weighing on its stock given lofty investor expectations. The analysts say that Amazon is still capacity constrained and growing capex. They also point out that AWS added the second-most revenue dollars quarter-over-quarter in its history despite these constraints. That occurred as its backlog grew $6 billion quarter-over-quarter, which is below peers, but year-over-year growth accelerated to 25%. "AWS lost ground to Azure in 2Q, but Cloud growth can be lumpy," the analysts say. "Though AWS is not seeing the benefit of ChatGPT usage, broader enterprise GenAI adoption remains a growth catalyst." Shares decline 7.6% to $216.23. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1104 ET - Palantir's $10 billion, 10-year contract with the U.S. Army sees the software company continuing to attach itself to safe, high-priority programs and contracts with the Defense Department that are not at risk of getting cut in the current government-spending climate, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. The company is well positioned in this spending environment and poised to actually get more of the Pentagon's information-technology budget dollars, the analysts say. This new deal should be a positive growth catalyst and give Palantir a bigger seat at the table globally, the analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1025 ET - Amazon's stock decline likely reflects Wall Street's frustration with its AWS performance, JP Morgan's Doug Anmuth and Bryan Smilek say in a research note. The internet retailer"s cloud-computing arm reported that sales climbed 17.5% in 2Q, but the analysts note the Street is likely frustrated with its growth, profit and AI positioning given the 30%+ growth at Microsoft and Google's cloud units, even as AWS comes off a much bigger base. "However, we continue to expect better AWS growth in 2H - 18.5% in 3Q and 19% in 4Q - and our overall estimates increase coming out of earnings, with higher stores profit offsetting lower AWS margins," say Anmuth and Smilek. Shares decline 6.3% to $219.47. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1020 ET - Apple posted solid beats across the board in the June quarter as customers raced to buy new iPhones and MacBooks before potential price increases from tariffs, but there's a dark cloud looming over the company, according to Wedbush in a note. "The AI Revolution is the biggest technology trend in 40 years, and right now Apple is watching this from a park bench drinking lemonade while every other big tech company is racing ahead like F1 drivers, building out their AI strategies and monetization plans," the analysts say. They think Apple should lean on M&A to bolster its AI ambitions, as "it's clear to us the innovations around AI are not going to come from Apple Park." The clock is ticking for CEO Tim Cook to find an answer to the company's AI problems, they write. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1015 ET - Palantir was awarded a $10 billion, 10-year contract that should prove to be an additional tailwind for the software company as the federal government accelerates its artificial-intelligence initiatives, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. The deal establishes a framework for the Army's future software and data needs by pooling together dozens of contracts into a single agreement with volume-based discounts, the analysts say. This should accelerate the delivery of proven commercial software to warfighters while removing contract and re-seller pass-through fees and reducing procurement timelines, they say. "Palantir [is] in the sweet spot to benefit from a tidal wave of federal spending on AI," the analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

0953 ET - Investor concerns about the intermediate-term trajectory of Amazon's cloud-computing arm's growth rate and margin could remain an overhang in the coming quarters, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. AWS reported that sales climbed 17.5% in 2Q but it fell short of Wall Street's expectations. The analysts note that investor concerns could linger if there's a perception that AWS is a laggard in the artificial intelligence space, particularly amid the momentum at Microsoft and Google's cloud units. "While we understand the reaction in shares after-hours, we do not believe the company's longer-term opportunity is impaired," say the analysts. Amazon is off nearly 7% in early trading. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0527 ET - Taiwan's tech sector and broader economic outlook might not face severe long-term consequences from U.S. tariffs, Janus Henderson Investors head of greater China equities Victoria Mio says. Global demand for AI infrastructure will boost Taiwan equities over the long term, Mio adds, but she remains vigilant on negotiations between the U.S. and Taiwan on tariffs, and Taiwan's economic indicators. The updated tariff rate for Taiwan is 20%, down from the previously announced 32%, but remains higher than the 15% for Japan and South Korea, Mio says. Taiwan's growth will likely slow in the second half of the year, rather than collapse as implied by the PMI trend, she adds. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0524 ET - China's gaming companies could further diversify account top-up channels to cut costs, HSBC analysts say in a research note. Chinese game makers have ramped up efforts to get users to top up their wallets to spend on mobile games through platforms such as WeChat and company websites rather than iOS and Android app stores to cut costs. Android app stores typically charge channel costs of up to 50% of grossing, while iOS app stores charge 30%. HSBC estimates payments from alternative channels have accounted for around 40%-50% of total mobile game grossing, and that this trend could continue. Meanwhile, they reckon shooting games could have more room to grow in China, with Tencent, which is a leading player in the genre, set to benefit. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0522 ET - Apple's latest quarterly figures caught investors by surprise as the iPhone maker defied seasonal trends, JPMorgan analysts write in a research note. Results for Apple's fiscal third quarter ended June 28 show a significant acceleration in revenue growth and iPhone sales, the analysts say. Strong iPhone sales are surprising in a quarter where the main concern for investors has generally been consumers waiting for the next generation of iPhones to be launched in September, they say. The tech giant reported $94.04 billion in total sales for the quarter, with the iPhone contributing $44.58 billion. Apple shares are up 1.9% premarket at $211.55. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0506 ET - Apple might have to navigate a host of challenges in the coming months even though its latest quarterly results show the company is faring better than feared, UBS analysts write in a note to clients. The iPhone maker is grappling with competition from local brands in China, analysts say. Apple also faces potential tariffs as an investigation by the Trump administration could result in specific levies for semiconductors, they add. The risk that the Department of Justice changes Apple's revenue agreement with Alphabet's Google when it is expected to rule in August also remains, analysts say. Apple shares are up 1.5% premarket at $210.73 after strong quarterly results. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

data.title: Supreme Critical Metals Inc. Provides Third Default Status Update
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / August 1, 2025 / Supreme Critical Metals Inc. (FWB:VR61) ("Supreme" or the "Company") provides its third bi-weekly Default Status Report under National Policy 12-203 Management Cease Trade Orders ("NP 12-203"), pursuant to which Supreme issued its original default announcement indicating that the filing of its annual audited financial statements, annual management's discussion and analysis and related certifications for the financial year ended February 28, 2025 (collectively, the "Required Filings"), would be delayed beyond the filing deadline of June 27, 2025. The Company reports that since it's news release dated July 18, 2025, there have been no material changes to the information contained therein that have not been disclosed, nor any failure by Supreme to fulfill its intentions as stated therein, other than as set forth below.

As a result of circumstances reasonably linked to the Company's delay in filing the Required Filings, in addition to the default to which this Default Status Report relates, the Company did not meet the deadline for filing its interim unaudited financial statements, interim management's discussion and analysis, and related certifications for the three-month period ended May 31, 2025 (collectively, the "Interim Filings"). As a result of the Company's delay in preparing the Required Filings, the Interim Filings could not be properly completed in accordance with Canadian securities law and regulation. Under the securities laws of the provinces in which Supreme is a reporting issuer, the Interim Filings were to be filed by July 30, 2025.

The Company's Board of Directors and management confirm that they are working expeditiously to meet the Company's obligations related to the filing of the Required Filings and the Interim Filings. The Company will file the Interim Filings within five business days of filing of the Required Filings.

Until the Required Filings and Interim Filings have been filed, the Company intends to continue to satisfy the provisions of the alternative information guidelines specified in NP 12-203 by issuing bi-weekly default status reports in the form of further press releases for so long as the Company remains in default of the financial statement filing requirement. In the event that the Company does not file the Required Filings and Interim Filings in a timely manner, the Canadian Securities Regulatory Authorities may impose an issuer cease trade order on the outstanding securities of the Company.

About Supreme Critical Metals Inc.

SUPREME CRITICAL METALS INC. is a publicly traded diversified investment corporation actively exploring and investigating multiple opportunities in lithium, copper, silver, and precious metals. The Company adheres to strategic guidelines that prioritize regions conducive to mining, supported by favourable government regulations and existing infrastructure.

For further information, please contact:

George Tsafalas, Director

Phone: Toll Free 1(778) 373-8578

E-mail: info@supremecritalmetals.com

www.supremecriticalmetals.com

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0759 ET - Shares in European pharmaceutical companies are mostly down, but off the session lows after President Trump took aim at the sector. "Investors in the healthcare sector got a dose of the blues after Trump wrote to various company bosses outlining how they should cut U.S. prescription drug prices or face a crackdown," says Russ Mould, investment director at AJ Bell. Trump sent letters to 17 global companies asking them to bring down the price of their drugs for U.S. consumers to the lowest price offered in other developed nations. Shares of Novo Nordisk, GSK, AstraZeneca and Sanofi all fell at the open, but have since pared much of their early losses. Novo Nordisk and AstraZeneca shares are down 0.1%, with GSK down 1% and Sanofi up 0.6%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0710 ET - Switzerland faces a significant hit to its economy from U.S. trade tariffs, Capital Economics' Adrian Prettejohn warns. The Swiss government said Friday it is "analyzing the situation" after President Trump said the U.S. would slap 39% duties on Swiss imports, which range from pharmaceuticals to luxury watches and machinery, though pharma products appear to be exempted for now from the higher tariffs. That threatens to reduce the export-oriented Swiss economy by some 0.6%, according to Capital Economics' calculations. And if pharma is eventually hit by 39% duties, the drag would be significantly more, Prettejohn says. The Swiss economy will continue to grow under those circumstances, but by a notably slower rate, he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0341 ET - European stocks fall sharply in early trade, led by losses in the healthcare and insurance sectors, after President Trump signed an executive order Thursday night significantly raising tariffs on scores of nations. Trump also sent letters to 17 big pharmaceutical companies asking them to lower costs of drugs for U.S. consumers. The Stoxx Europe 600 falls 1%, with the insurance and healthcare sector indexes down 1.6% and 1.5%, respectively. Germany's DAX, France's CAC 40 and Italy's FTSE MIB all drop more than 1%. Markets are closed in Switzerland--which will be hit with a 39% tariff, Trump's order said--due to a local holiday. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0328 ET - Shares of European pharmaceutical companies fell in early trade after U.S. President Trump sent out letters Thursday asking them to cut the costs of drugs for U.S. customers to the lowest offered in other developed nations.Shares of GSK, AstraZeneca, Novo Nordisk, and Sanofi were down 1.7%, 2.8%, 4.2% and 1.2%, respectively. The Stoxx Europe 600 healthcare index was down 1.8%. The President said he would use "every tool in our arsenal" to protect American families from high prices if companies didn't act voluntarily. (ian.walker@wsj.com.)

0255 ET - Asean market watchers will keep a close eye on sector-specific tariffs, RHB Bank group chief economist and head of market research Barnabas Gan says in a report. These tariffs pose downside risks for regional countries, especially those with export-heavy industries, Gan writes. "Malaysia and Singapore appear most exposed to potential semiconductor-related tariffs, while pharmaceutical-related measures would likely weigh heavily on Singapore and Thailand." Thailand's automotive sector and Indonesia's electric-vehicle battery segment could also be hurt by any new levies targeting the broader auto value chain, he says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0051 ET - The U.S.'s 19% tariff on Malaysian exports is slightly negative for the glove sector, as it will narrow Malaysia's pricing advantage, says TA Securities analyst Tan Kong Jin in a note. Customers are expected to continue resisting cost-sharing as oversupply persists, while Malaysian glove makers' plant utilization rates remain below optimal levels, he notes. Trade uncertainty also lingers, with ongoing U.S.-China tariff negotiations. Tan doesn't rule out target price cuts on Malaysian glove stocks if China, Malaysia's biggest competitor in the glove industry, secures a more favorable trade deal. TA Securities maintains an underweight rating on the sector as it expects global oversupply to last at least three more years. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0027 ET - China's WuXi AppTec could see increased demand for its contract research, development and manufacturing services over the next few years, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a note. They expect surging biopharmaceutical investment to drive demand, leading to earnings growth. WuXi could improve profit margins from 2H, given higher capacity utilization and the sale of its loss-making cell-and-gene therapy business. The analysts raise their 2025-2027 revenue compound annual growth rate estimate to 14.8%, citing stronger-than-expected results from its contract drug-development segment. They also forecast a 22.1% adjusted earnings CAGR over 2025-2027. UOB KH raises its rating to buy from hold with a higher target price of HK$146.00 from HK$70.00 on its H-shares. Shares are 2.2% lower atHK$103.40. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1352 ET - Gold futures rise to start the month, peaking over the $3,400 per troy ounce mark for the first time in over a week. Gold was boosted this morning by a less-than-expected July payrolls reading, and supported throughout the day as gold demand remains strong, according to data from the World Gold Council. "Global gold ETFs have further upside potential in our view, but might face some near-term headwinds before resuming growth in 2H," says the group in its latest research note. While gold rose, the U.S. dollar slid, falling 0.7%. Front-month gold futures settles up 0.4% to $3,347.70 a troy ounce. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0628 ET - Erste Group Bank's strong results should please the market, Citi says in a research note. The Austrian lender's second-quarter print surpassed expectations. Moreover, the bank upgraded its guidance, saying it now expects its return on tangible equity for the year to be above 15% on higher net interest income and lower provisions. This, along with the stronger-than-expected capital build over the period should be well received, analysts write. Shares rose as much as 3% before paring gains and dipping into the red by midday. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0624 ET - AIB Group may have disappointed investors by sticking with its guidance and not launching a share-buyback program, which some may have expected, Citi says in a research note. In its half-year results, the Irish lender posted an in-line print with strong capital, an interim dividend, and an increase in its structural hedge, analysts write. The hedge is an accounting mechanism that mitigates the impact of interest-rate moves on its top line. An upgrade to the hedge could provide upside to net interest income estimates for the next couple of years. "We would view any potential share weakness as an enhanced buying opportunity," they say. Shares fall 2.5% to 6.78 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0518 ET - AXA's latest acquisition of a direct insurer in Italy is interesting, Citi says in a research note. The French insurer said it is buying a majority stake in Prima for 500 million euros. "This is an impressive company and 11x multiple seems reasonable," analyst James Shuck writes, noting the deal follows its recent acquisition of Italian insurer Nobis. The two deals show how AXA is seeking to leverage insurtechs to gain scale in Italy, he adds. "We like the Prima deal but we do note some industry skepticism around margins and note that Axa doesn't have the best track record in acquiring direct motor insurers," he adds. AXA also reported first-half results that were roughly in line with views except for net profit, which was dented by currency fluctuations. Shares fall 6% to 40.1 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0426 ET - Shares in insurer AXA fall as much as 7% before paring back some losses after the stronger euro ate into the French group's earnings. The stock is the worst performer of the Stoxx 600 insurance index. Keefe, Bruyette & Woods analyst William Hawkins notes that the headline earnings miss against consensus stands out and is primarily driven by foreign exchange. "We should know this already. It is more prominent in our minds as a 5% headwind to future earnings growth than we thought previously," he writes in a research note. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0307 ET - London Stock Exchange Group's progress has been overlooked, JP Morgan Cazenove analysts write in a note. The negative market reaction to the company's recent performance was overdone, they add. The analysts recognize concerns over competition and hurdles in Data & Analytics unit. However, they believe LSEG's solid constant-currency growth and improved operating leverage--including the lifted Ebitda margin guidance--have been overlooked. LSEG continues to be a unique asset in the European financial sector, with diversified revenue stream across industries that are benefiting from long-term growth trends, they say. The company's strong cash generation further enhance its appeal, they add. Shares are up 0.8% at 9,330 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

2042 ET - Judo Capital's achievement in hitting its annual gross-loan target elicits a sigh of relief from Morgans analyst Nathan Lead. He tells clients in a note that the Australian business lender's A$12.465 billion gross-loan balance at June 30 should give investors increased confidence. Judo had guided for A$12.4 billion-A$12.6 billion. He points out that Judo recorded its largest-ever monthly rise in loan growth in June, but cautions against reading too much into it due to the seasonality of drawdowns. It's also positive that term deposits grew by more than loans, even if the balance fell short of expectations. Morgans keeps an accumulate rating and A$1.75 target price on the stock. Shares are down 0.5% at A$1.5075. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1338 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by five this week to 410, or 72 fewer than a year ago, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. The 14th straight weekly drop leaves oil rigs at their lowest level since September 2021, as drilling efficiency, capital discipline and relatively low oil prices keep operators from ramping up activity. Rigs directed at natural gas increased for a third straight week, rising by two to 124 or 26 more than a year ago. "With the rig increases, we now forecast gas supply/demand balances narrowing to the 5-year average in 2H25, but remaining slightly oversupplied through 1Q26," analysts at UBS said in a report. "Our new timetable for balances to go undersupplied is now pushed out to mid-2026." (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1235 ET - Crude futures extend losses after U.S. July payrolls come in below expectations and job gains for the previous two months are revised down sharply. That adds to demand concerns with U.S. import tariffs set to take effect Aug. 7. "This is definitely a macro-driven selloff," says CIBC Private Wealth US senior energy trader Rebecca Babin. The tariff headlines "are shaping up to be a little bit on the worse side of what was expected." On the positive side, the soft employment data puts September in play for a Fed rate cut, "but weakening economic numbers is something that the market has really been fearing," Babin adds. Also on the bearish side, OPEC+ is expected to agree to another large output increase in September at its Sunday meeting. WTI is down 2.8% at $67.33 a barrel and Brent is off 2.9% at $69.66. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1053 ET - Overall, Imperial Oil had a good quarter, reckons Raymond James's Michael Barth. The analyst says the energy company managed a modest 4% beat on funds from operations over consensus estimates on what largely appears to be slightly stronger production. Still, given that Imperial is one of the best performing large-cap Canadian energy names, Barth is skeptical the latest quarter will be enough to drive meaningful outperformance against its peer group from here. Shares are down 1% at C$114.34. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

0927 ET - Enbridge may have affirmed its Ebitda guidance for 2025 where investors may have wanted to see a lift given a strong 1H performance, but its results overall were solid, Jefferies' Sam Burwell says. The energy company's 2Q Ebitda beat the consensus forecast by 2.5%, driven by its U.S. gas transmission business, and it ended the quarter with debt leverage of 4.7 times, the analyst notes. Liquids earnings were in line with what Burwell anticipated, the renewables business was slightly below his forecast, but has transmission offered a solid beat and gas distribution was modestly ahead of his forecast. Jefferies has a one-year forward target of C$72 on the shares, which were last at C$62.75. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

0912 ET - Oil futures flit between small gains and losses as the U.S. tariff deadline passes, with tariffs due to kick in on Aug. 7, fueling concerns about demand. "Values are being supported by the need to maintain risk premium for possibly another week on the chance that secondary tariffs may be placed on importers of Russian oil," Ritterbusch says in a note. "Oil price downside in response to expected weakening in economic and oil demand growth paths is being limited mainly to the product markets." WTI is up 0.1% at $69.30 a barrel, and Brent is down 0.1% at $71.65 as October moves to the front of the curve. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

2127 ET - Origin Energy's near-term spending will probably be larger when its part-owned Reedy Creek and Iron Bark gas projects are approved, according to Macquarie analysts. They flag that costs will be structurally higher in the coming three years as the Australia Pacific LNG venture starts replacing production. They tell clients in a note that they anticipate negative cash-flow impacts from fiscal 2027 onwards. Macquarie keeps a neutral rating and A$10.94 target price on the stock, which is up 0.5% at A$11.725. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2014 ET - Oil edges higher in the early Asian session, supported by supply-disruption concerns. President Trump has given Russian President Putin an Aug. 8 deadline to reach a cease-fire with Ukraine, shortening a 50-day deadline he had set. The deadline for Russia to end its invasion and a full ceasefire has aided some worries over supply disruptions, says Janiv Shah, vice president at Rystad Energy, in an email. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are up 0.1% at $69.33/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.1% higher at $71.80/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1852 ET - Beach Energy's quarterly update had a sting in its tail for Euroz Hartleys. While Beach achieved FY 2025 guidance, it also signaled several negative developments. Among them: Faster-than-anticipated field decline in the Otway Basin, prolonged flooding impacts in the Cooper Basin, and a delay to first gas from the second stage of its Waitsia project. Euroz Hartleys cuts a forecast for annual production to 19.7 million BOE, from 23.0 million BOE. It also pares expectations for Ebitda, operating cash flow and dividends. Euroz Hartleys lowers its price target by 13%, to A$1.32/share, noting that drilling of the Beharra Springs Deep 3 gas well suggests the field in the Perth Basin has lower accessible reserves than previously thought. Beach fell 9.3% yesterday, to A$1.17. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1426 ET - Kimberly-Clark's efforts to adapt some of its best product features for its value tier are starting to pay off, CEO Mike Hsu says during a call with analysts. The company saw organic sales up 3.9% in its latest quarter, driven primarily by demand for new products, he says. Going forward, the company plans to bring its innovation efforts to the premium and mid-tier segments, which should drive further growth into the back half of the year, he says. "The consumption is very durable in our categories, and that's really driven by excellent innovation and excellent execution," he says. Kimberly-Clark up 4.5%. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1352 ET - Gold futures rise to start the month, peaking over the $3,400 per troy ounce mark for the first time in over a week. Gold was boosted this morning by a less-than-expected July payrolls reading, and supported throughout the day as gold demand remains strong, according to data from the World Gold Council. "Global gold ETFs have further upside potential in our view, but might face some near-term headwinds before resuming growth in 2H," says the group in its latest research note. While gold rose, the U.S. dollar slid, falling 0.7%. Front-month gold futures settles up 0.4% to $3,347.70 a troy ounce. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0906 ET - The July jobs report showing fewer new jobs added than economists expected sends gold futures up, pushing the most-active contract 1.3% higher after starting the day virtually flat. The government also said revisions for May and June were larger than normal. The report is being taken as a sign the labor market may be weaker than previously believed. The data is likely to add more pressure for Fed to cut rates in September. Fed Chair Powell's comments after leaving interest rates unchanged Wednesday had been keeping a lid on gold, with Powell not willing to signal if a cut was coming next month. "Powell thus gave the impression that everything was still open," says Commerzbank in a note. The dollar has turned weaker, with the WSJ index down 0.8%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

2154 ET - Productivity and simplification are key for Rio Tinto's next CEO, Simon Trott, to "return Rio to its former glory," Macquarie analysts say in a note. The analysts say Rio Tinto's minerals arm, which includes commodities such as titanium dioxide, borates and diamonds, undid all the good work of its copper division in 1H. "We think a portfolio simplification mantra could deliver value via reducing earnings volatility and risk." Macquarie raises its target on Rio's Australian shares by 3% to A$109/share. The stock is up 0.6% at A$112.32. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2143 ET - A rebased commodity-price graph in Rio Tinto's 1H result shows copper and aluminum trading around long-term averages, but iron ore and lithium trading below. That prompts Macquarie analysts to contemplate the possible price risks it could face in those industries. "For those with long memories, they may recall when iron ore struggled to exceed $40/ton (nominal) prior to 2005 and lump VIU's [value-in-use] drove slim producer profits," say the analysts. At that time, the iron-ore market was experiencing oversupply from new basins, volume-based industry rivalry and strong customer buying power, they say. "We hope history does not repeat itself." (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2117 ET - Efforts to better Mineral Resources' disclosure and capital allocation "have certainly improved MIN's investment proposition," Macquarie says in a note. The miner also appears poised to benefit from lower lithium production costs in the future, according to the bank. Macquarie raises its target on the stock by 32% to A$29/share. Yet, a 40% surge in the miner's shares since the start of July has already pushed its value past that target. Macquarie downgrades the stock to underperform from neutral, citing valuation and the potential for weaker iron-ore prices over the next couple of years. Mineral Resources is up 5.1% at A$30.04. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2053 ET - IGO's lowered liquidity threshold, focus on productivity and planned impairment of the Kwinana refinery "all pave the way for a focus on shareholder returns while it navigates market volatility," Macquarie says. The bank raises its target on the stock by 11% to A$5.00/share. It reiterates an outperform rating. IGO's 4Q result is mixed, with a production miss offset by a sales beat, Macquarie says. IGO is up 1.7% at A$4.505, erasing a small part of this week's more-than-10% loss. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2033 ET - Now could be a good time for Kingsgate Consolidated to sell its Nueva Esperanza silver project in Chile, Canaccord Genuity suggests. That's because silver prices have surged 30% so far this year. Nueva Esperanza has a resource of 134 million oz of silver, grading at 106 grams per ton of ore. "Should the project be divested, we think this could be a windfall and valuation catalyst for Kingsgate," analyst Reg Spencer says. It points to Andean Silver as a potential benchmark for valuing the project. Andean Silver, with a market value of A$232 million, owns the Cerro Bayo project in Chile that has a resource of 120 million oz of silver. "While Cerro Bayo is more advanced with established infrastructure, we think it could provide a valuation yardstick for any divestment by Kingsgate," Canaccord says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1856 ET [Dow Jones]--Global softwood kraft pulp market conditions are expected to stay weak in the third quarter, Canfor Pulp Products said. "The subdued demand for bleached kraft paper in North America observed at the end of the first quarter and throughout the second quarter is anticipated to persist into the third quarter," Canfor Pulp said. The outlook is mostly attributed to ongoing uncertainties related to Canada-US trade relations, and general global economic pressures, the company added. Purchasing activity, especially from China, is expected to remain soft during the traditionally slower summer period, Canfor said, adding "as a result, global pulp producer inventories are forecast to remain well above the balanced range through the third quarter of 2025." (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0538 ET - IAG's strong balance sheet gives the airline group the optionality to continue to add brands or grow dividends, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The group--which operates British Airways, Iberia, and Level among other cariers--has margins that are higher than prepandemic levels and can continue growing as long as long-haul capacity recovers and BA's self-help opportunities materialize, Jefferies says. Its outlook is robust and updated guidance implies a rise in EBIT estimates of 10% to around 5.2 billion euros, Jefferies says. "We see value in IAG shares, given further self-help opportunities, relatively small carbon headwinds and strong cash generation," Jefferies says.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0402 ET - IAG's 250 million-euro EBIT beat in 2Q is enough to drive upgrades of 5% to 6%, RBC Capital Markets analyst Ruairi Cullinane says in a note. The airline group lowered its total fuel cost guidance for the year by 5.3% to 7.1 billion euros, which could make consensus estimates rise by even more, he says. The company is 57% booked for the second half with revenue in line with last year, compared with 29% booked for the 2H last year. "We think IAG has a stronger outlook than given credit for, given a supportive capacity backdrop on the Atlantic, pricing potential from BA product measures, and given scope for business travel recovery to step up further," he says. Shares are up 1.3% at 385.50 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0332 ET - Maruti Suzuki India appears optimistic on the demand outlook for 2Q FY 2026, Elara Securities (India) analysts say in a research report. The automaker sees demand picking up in 2Q, led by the early festival season, good monsoon and robust rural demand, the analysts note. The Indian company's two new launches within sports utility vehicles are on track, while dealer inventory is normalized at 33 days as of end-1Q. The key catalyst for the stock is a recovery in domestic demand in India, which will be keenly monitored by the brokerage during the festive season. The brokerage maintains the stock's accumulate rating, but raises the target price to INR14,279.00 from INR13,961.00 to reflect valuation roll-forward. Shares are 1.9% lower at INR12,393.90. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0303 ET - IAG surpassed 2Q expectations with a 17% beat on operating profit, Bernstein analysts Alex Irving and Antoine Madre say on a note. Year-on-year passenger revenue per available seat-kilometer----a key revenue metric measuring the ratio between an airline's revenue and its passenger carrying capacity--grew 2.6% to 8.55 euros, they note. Still, the airline group's revenue seem close to last year's, which appears to imply some PRASK decline into 2H, albeit against tough comparisons, the analysts say. The company is likely to see changes to consensus earnings on these results, Bernstein says. Shares are up 2.5% at 390.10 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0300 ET - NIO's shares rose sharply in Hong Kong on Friday, thanks to positive initial feedback surrounding its new model, the Onvo L90, released late Thursday, Nomura's analyst Joel Ying said. Some analysts are comparing Onvo L90 with Li Auto's SUV model i8. Nomura thinks the Onvo L90 looks more attractive in terms of hardware configuration, but still needs to evaluate the market's trends. Morningstar's Vincent Sun thinks L90 is more attractive in terms of pricing, considering its large size and battery leasing option. Also, initial demand for Li Auto's i8 may not be spectacular when it launches at year end as it is priced about CNY300,000, Sun added. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0255 ET - Asean market watchers will keep a close eye on sector-specific tariffs, RHB Bank group chief economist and head of market research Barnabas Gan says in a report. These tariffs pose downside risks for regional countries, especially those with export-heavy industries, Gan writes. "Malaysia and Singapore appear most exposed to potential semiconductor-related tariffs, while pharmaceutical-related measures would likely weigh heavily on Singapore and Thailand." Thailand's automotive sector and Indonesia's electric-vehicle battery segment could also be hurt by any new levies targeting the broader auto value chain, he says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0254 ET - Pirelli's cut to its full-year revenue guidance aligns the company's expectations with consensus estimates, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. The Italian tire maker now expects to generate revenue of 6.7 billion to 6.8 billion euros for the year, down from its previous range of 6.8 billion to 7 billion euros, citing foreign-exchange headwinds. Volume growth should accelerate in the second half and the effects of pricing and sales mix continue to help top-line growth, likely aided by some U.S. price increases, Bernstein says. "The impact of U.S. tariffs is as expected, and [the full-year] impact may even be slightly lower than embedded in the guide after the EU 15% tariff deal," the analysts say. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0155 ET - Daimler Truck Holding cut its full-year expectations due to uncertainty clouding its North America business, and added a caveat to its outlook that suggests it remains at risk of further downgrades, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. The German truck and bus maker said its new guidance assumes the U.S.-Mexico-Canada trade agreement continues. However, a probe started by the U.S. government might change this, which would hurt Daimler, Bernstein say. The company lowered its adjusted operating profit expectations by 18% at the mid-point of its guidance range, and the mid-point of its new outlook sits 7% below consensus estimates, Bernstein says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0116 ET - Daimler Truck Holding's cut to its expectations for North America didn't come as a surprise, but the magnitude of the downgrade is likely to disappoint most and points to lower prices, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. "The extent of the cut also--worryingly--implies weakening pricing: the old industrial guide implied a revenue per unit of 111,000 euros, while the new guide implies 107,000 euros," the analysts say. The German truck and bus maker's guidance downgrade overshadows strong second-quarter results, with adjusted earnings topping consensus forecasts, Bernstein says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0056 ET - NIO's Onvo L90 seems to offer better value and user satisfaction than Li Auto's i8, Citi analysts say in a note. The Onvo L90 has a starting price of 265,800 yuan, compared with 321,800 yuan for the Li i8. Citi thinks the monthly run-rate for NIO's new car could reach 8,000 to 9,000 units, slightly ahead of market consensus, thanks to its attractive pricing and rich interior features. The L90 is now a clear standout among large battery-electric SUVs on the Chinese market, Citi says. It maintains a buy rating on NIO with a target price of $8.10. ADRs last closed at $4.87. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2158 ET - HD Hyundai Heavy Industries could gain from a recovery in U.S. demand for liquefied natural gas carriers likely in late 2H of 2025 or early 1H of 2026, Daiwa Capital analysts Mike Oh and Daeho Son say. Final investment decisions on U.S. LNG projects are likely to proceed without major disruptions, the analysts write in a note. They raise their EPS forecasts for the South Korean shipbuilder by 29% each for 2025 and 2026 to reflect its backlog order mix that is improving at a faster-than-expected pace. The company's new contract wins so far this year have come in at $5.5 billion, about 70% of its $7.7 billion annual target, they note. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Elme Communities expected to post a loss of 4 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Elme Communities , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Bethesda Maryland-based company is expected to report a 2.2% increase in revenue to $61.42 million from $60.1 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 3 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Elme Communities is for a loss of 4 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Elme Communities is $18.50, about 18.5% above its last closing price of $15.08

data.title: GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., a leading global investment banking, securities, and investment management firm, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements, showcasing the firm's robust performance across various business segments and its strategic initiatives aimed at driving future growth.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $14,583 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year, driven by strong performance in market making and investment management.

Net Interest Income: $3,104 million, showing a substantial rise compared to the prior year, indicating improved interest rate conditions.

Total Operating Expenses: $9,241 million, highlighting effective cost management despite increased compensation and benefits expenses.

Net Earnings: $3,723 million, demonstrating robust profitability with a notable increase from the previous year.

Net Earnings Applicable to Common Shareholders: $3,473 million, indicating strong earnings growth and shareholder value.

Basic EPS: $11.03, reflecting a significant improvement in earnings per share compared to the previous year.

Diluted EPS: $10.91, showcasing enhanced profitability and efficient capital management.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. operates through three main business segments: Global Banking & Markets, Asset & Wealth Management, and Platform Solutions. Each segment contributes to the firm's diverse revenue streams, with specific services tailored to different client needs.

Global Banking & Markets: This segment provides a wide range of services including strategic advisory assignments, mergers and acquisitions, equity and debt underwriting, and market-making activities. It serves a diverse group of corporations, financial institutions, investment funds, and governments.

Asset & Wealth Management: This segment focuses on managing assets and offering investment products across major asset classes. It caters to both institutional and individual clients, providing investment management services, wealth advisory solutions, and brokerage transactions.

Platform Solutions: This segment includes the issuance of credit cards through partnerships, transaction banking, and other services such as deposit-taking and payment solutions for corporate and institutional clients.

Geographical Performance: The firm maintains a global presence with offices in all major financial centers around the world, allowing it to serve a large and diversified client base effectively.

Future Outlook: The firm continues to focus on expanding its market presence and enhancing its service offerings across all business segments. It aims to leverage its global network and expertise to drive growth and deliver value to its clients.

SEC Filing: GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC [ GS ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: Extra Space Storage Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Extra Space Storage Inc., a leading provider of self-storage solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance and significant operational achievements, driven by strategic acquisitions and effective revenue management systems.

Financial Highlights

Extra Space Storage Inc. reported impressive financial results for the second quarter of 2025:

  • Total Revenues: $841.6 million, an increase of 3.8% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by acquisitions and an increase in the number of stores operated.
  • Net Income: $262.7 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year, attributed to higher revenues and effective cost management.
  • Net Income Attributable to Common Stockholders: $249.7 million, up from $185.9 million in the prior year, indicating strong performance in core operations.
  • Earnings Per Common Share - Basic: $1.18, compared to $0.88 in the previous year, showing improved profitability per share.
  • Earnings Per Common Share - Diluted: $1.18, consistent with the basic EPS, reflecting no significant dilution effects.

Business Highlights

In addition to its financial success, Extra Space Storage Inc. achieved several key operational milestones:

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in two main segments: self-storage operations and tenant reinsurance. Self-storage operations generated $721,004,000 in revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2025, while tenant reinsurance contributed $88,572,000.
  • Geographical Performance: The company owns or manages a total of 4,179 stores across 43 states and Washington, D.C. The largest concentration of stores is in Texas, with 537 stores totaling 43,094,288 net rentable square feet.
  • Sales Units: As of June 30, 2025, approximately 2,465,000 tenants were leasing storage units, primarily on a month-to-month basis, providing flexibility to increase rental rates over time.
  • New Production Launches: The company acquired 45 wholly-owned stores during the six months ended June 30, 2025, expanding its operational footprint.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in both self-storage operations and tenant reinsurance segments, leveraging its industry-leading revenue management systems to optimize rental rates and occupancy levels.

SEC Filing: Extra Space Storage Inc. [ EXR ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: EVEREST GROUP, LTD. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
EVEREST GROUP, LTD., a leading global reinsurance and insurance provider, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market conditions.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $8,754 million, reflecting an increase from $8,360 million in the prior year period, driven by higher premiums earned and net investment income.

Net Income (Loss): $890 million, a decrease from $1,457 million in the prior year period, impacted by higher incurred losses and loss adjustment expenses.

Earnings Per Common Share (Basic): $20.93, compared to $33.57 in the prior year period, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Earnings Per Common Share (Diluted): $20.93, consistent with the basic earnings per share, indicating no dilution effect.

Comprehensive Income (Loss): $1,707 million, an increase from $1,230 million in the prior year period, primarily due to improvements in other comprehensive income components.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates through two main segments: Reinsurance and Insurance. The Reinsurance segment writes worldwide property and casualty reinsurance and specialty lines of business, while the Insurance segment writes property and casualty insurance directly and through brokers.

Geographical Performance: The Reinsurance operation is conducted in the U.S., Bermuda, Ireland, Canada, Singapore, the U.K., and Switzerland. The Insurance operation is conducted in the U.S., Bermuda, Canada, Europe, Singapore, and South America, with offices in multiple countries including Australia, the Netherlands, France, Germany, Italy, and Spain.

Segment Underwriting Results: For the three months ended June 30, 2025, the Reinsurance segment reported an underwriting gain of $436 million, while the Insurance segment reported a loss of $18 million. The Other segment, which includes run-off business, reported a loss of $33 million.

Operational Changes: During the fourth quarter of 2024, the company revised its classification and presentation of certain run-off business into a new segment called 'Other', which includes results from the sports and leisure business sold in October 2024.

Future Outlook: The company continues to evaluate its segments as the business evolves and may further refine its segments and financial performance measures. The management is focused on maintaining strong underwriting results and managing risks effectively across its operations.

SEC Filing: EVEREST GROUP, LTD. [ EG ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: BLUE OWL CAPITAL INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital Inc., a leading alternative asset management firm, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, highlighting significant financial and operational achievements for the second quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's robust revenue growth, strategic business initiatives, and future outlook in a challenging market environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues, Net: $703.1 million, an increase of $153.3 million compared to the same period last year, primarily driven by higher management fees across all platforms.

Net Income Attributable to Blue Owl Capital Inc.: $17.4 million, a decrease of $16.5 million from the previous year, impacted by higher compensation and benefits expenses.

Income Before Income Taxes: $84.5 million, a decrease of $71.8 million from the prior year, reflecting increased expenses and other losses.

Fee-Related Earnings (FRE): $358.3 million, an increase of $61.9 million, driven by higher FRE revenues in Credit, Real Assets, and GP Strategic Capital.

Distributable Earnings: $323.0 million, an increase of $50.0 million, supported by higher FRE revenues despite increased expenses.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported significant growth in management fees across its three main platforms: Credit, Real Assets, and GP Strategic Capital. The Credit platform saw an increase due to continued fundraising and deployment of capital within new and existing direct lending products. Real Assets experienced growth primarily due to digital infrastructure products, while GP Strategic Capital benefited from fundraising in its sixth flagship minority equity stakes product.

Geographical Performance: The business environment was impacted by global market volatility, particularly due to tariffs affecting economic growth and trade dynamics. Despite these challenges, the company's management-fee-centric model and base of Permanent Capital supported earnings resiliency and business growth.

Sales Units: The company reported a record fundraising quarter, bringing in $13.9 billion of new capital commitments during the second quarter of 2025, resulting in $54.6 billion of total capital raised over the last twelve months.

New Product Launches: The Real Assets platform held the first close for the seventh vintage of its net lease strategy, less than 15 months after the final close of the prior vintage. This includes financing the largest data center project in the U.S., highlighting the company's focus on innovation and income-oriented products.

Future Outlook: The company plans to pursue strategic acquisitions and investments to accelerate growth and broaden product offerings. data.title: Bessent says US has 'makings of a deal' with China
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Friday that he believed that Washington has the makings of a deal with China and that he was "optimistic" about the path forward.

"This week's negotiations in Stockholm have advanced our talks with China, and I believe that we have the makings of a deal that will benefit both of our great nations," Bessent said in a post on X that was subsequently deleted.

"I am optimistic about the path forward," he added.

A Treasury Department spokesperson said the post was being reposted because the images attached to it had not uploaded correctly. The spokesperson also noted that the language in the post was in line with what Bessent had said in various media interviews this week.

In an interview with CNBC on Thursday, Bessent said the United States believes it has the makings of a trade deal with China, but it is "not 100% done."

U.S. negotiators "pushed back quite a bit" over two days of trade talks with the Chinese in Stockholm this week, Bessent told CNBC.

China is facing an August 12 deadline to reach a durable tariff agreement with President Donald Trump's administration, after Beijing and Washington reached preliminary deals in May and June to end escalating tit-for-tat tariffs and a cut-off of rare earth minerals.

data.title: Ukraine's Supporters Plan New NATO Fund to Buy U.S. Weapons — WSJ
data.description:
By Lara Seligman and Michael R. Gordon

Ukraine's supporters will set up a new NATO holding account to allow allies to buy billions of dollars of U.S. weapons for Ukraine, part of President Trump's latest scheme to arm Kyiv, according to three Western officials.

The creation of the new account marks the first tangible step in making Trump's vow to have North Atlantic Treaty Organization allies pay for U.S. weapons for Ukraine a reality. Trump and NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte announced the deal last month but didn't provide details.

"We've made a deal today where we are going to be sending them weapons and they're going to be paying for them," Trump said, sitting next to Rutte in the Oval Office in July. "We're not buying it, but we will manufacture it, and they're going to be paying for it."

The decision is a policy shift for the U.S., which under President Joe Biden donated weapons from its own inventories to Ukraine. The end goal is to get Russia to the negotiating table and end the conflict, according to a senior NATO military official.

The idea is for NATO allies to voluntarily donate funds into the new account, the three people said. The Supreme Allied Commander for Europe, Gen. Alexus Grynkewich, will be responsible for vetting Ukraine's weapons requirements. The money in the account will be used to pay for American-made or supplied weapons sent to Kyiv after Ukraine's battlefield demands are balanced against the U.S. military's needs.

Ukraine's supporters aim to initially spend a total of $10 billion on weapons for Ukraine, one of the people said, most of which are expected to come from U.S. defense industry production lines.

Trump has $3.85 billion left over in presidential drawdown authority from the Biden administration to send weapons to Ukraine directly from U.S. stockpiles. The Republican chairmen of the Senate Armed Services and Foreign Relations Committees have proposed legislation that would enable the Pentagon to be reimbursed by European nations for any arms that might be provided.

Deliveries of American weapons to Kyiv that were authorized by the Biden administration are still flowing across the border from Poland. Some of those weapons — primarily munitions like Patriot air-defense interceptors — were paused in June as part of a Pentagon review of U.S. munitions stockpiles. But those deliveries have since resumed, officials said.

As part of the effort to arm Ukraine, the U.S. struck an agreement with Berlin under which Germany would send additional Patriot air-defense systems to Kyiv. Ukraine is set to receive the first two of these systems in the coming days, the German government announced Friday. In exchange, Germany will be the first nation to receive the newest Patriot systems off the U.S. production line at "an accelerated pace," according to a release from the German government.

To facilitate this agreement, the Pentagon moved Germany ahead of Switzerland in the queue for the next Patriots, The Wall Street Journal previously reported. The U.S. plans to reshuffle future Patriot deliveries as additional countries sign on to send the systems from their arsenals to Ukraine, a senior U.S. official said.

Write to Lara Seligman at lara.seligman@wsj.com and Michael R. Gordon at michael.gordon@wsj.com

data.title: Sanatana Announces Market Making Services Agreement with Independent Trading Group
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - August 1, 2025) - Sanatana Resources Inc. ("Sanatana" or the "Company") has entered into a market making services agreement (the "Agreement") with Independent Trading Group (ITG), Inc. ("ITG"), effective August 1, 2025. Under the Agreement, ITG will provide market-making services to the Company (the "Services") in accordance with the rules and policies of the TSX Venture Exchange (the "TSXV"). The Services will include trading shares of the Company on the TSXV with the objective of maintaining a reasonable market and improving the liquidity of the Company's shares.

The Agreement is for an initial term of one month (the "Initial Term") and will automatically renew for subsequent periods of one month after the Initial Term (each subsequent renewal being an "Additional Term"). The Agreement may be terminated if either party provides written notice of termination to the other party at least 30 days prior to the end of the Initial Term or Additional Term. As consideration for the Services, the Company will pay ITG a cash fee of $5,000 (plus all applicable taxes), payable on the first business day of every month for the duration of the Agreement.

There are no performance obligations contained in the Agreement, and ITG will not receive common shares, stock options, or any other form of equity in the Company as compensation. ITG and the Company are arm's length, unrelated and unaffiliated entities and at the time of the Agreement, neither ITG nor its principals have an interest, directly or indirectly, in the securities of the Company.

About Independent Trading Group (ITG), Inc.

Independent Trading Group (ITG), Inc. is a Toronto based CIRO dealer-member that specializes in market making, liquidity provision, agency execution, ultra-low latency connectivity, and bespoke algorithmic trading solutions. Established in 1992, with a focus on market structure, execution and trading, ITG has leveraged its own proprietary technology to deliver high quality liquidity provision and execution services to a broad array of public issuers and institutional investors. ITG's head office is located at 33 Yonge Street, Suite 420, Toronto, Ontario, M5E 1G4.

About Sanatana Resources Inc.

Sanatana Resources Inc. is a mineral exploration and development company focused on high-impact properties in Canada. With an award-winning technical team and experienced management and board of directors, Sanatana is based in Vancouver and is listed on the TSX Venture Exchange .

data.title: SEC Creates Task Force to Tap Artificial Intelligence for Enhanced Innovation and Efficiency Across the Agency
data.description:
Washington, D.C.--(Newsfile Corp. - August 1, 2025) - The Securities and Exchange Commission today announced the launch of a task force on artificial intelligence (AI) that will spearhead the agency’s efforts to enhance innovation and efficiency in its operations through the responsible use of AI. Valerie Szczepanik, who has been named the SEC’s Chief AI Officer, is leading the task force.

Recognizing the transformative potential of AI, the SEC’s AI Task Force will accelerate AI integration to bolster the SEC’s mission. It will centralize the agency’s efforts and enable internal cross-agency and cross-disciplinary collaboration to navigate the AI lifecycle, remove barriers to progress, focus on AI applications that maximize benefits, and maintain governance. The task force will support innovation from the SEC’s divisions and offices and facilitate responsible AI integration across the agency.

“The AI Task Force will empower staff across the SEC with AI-enabled tools and systems to responsibly augment the staff’s capacity, accelerate innovation, and enhance efficiency and accuracy,” said SEC Chairman Paul S. Atkins. “By ingraining innovation into our culture SEC-wide, we will further our mission to protect investors, maintain fair, orderly, and efficient markets, and facilitate capital formation.”

Ms. Szczepanik said, “I thank Chairman Atkins for selecting me to lead this important initiative for the agency. The AI Task Force will aim to centralize and align efforts to advance AI-enabled transformation across the entire Commission. I look forward to accelerating work already underway in the SEC’s divisions and offices to build enterprise capacity for AI innovation and implement AI solutions that are trustworthy, effective, and mission enhancing.”

Ms. Szczepanik previously was Director of the SEC’s Strategic Hub for Innovation and Financial Technology. She was the Senior Advisor for Digital Assets and Innovation and an Associate Director in the SEC’s Division of Corporation Finance. Before that, she served as Assistant Director in the Division of Enforcement. Ms. Szczepanik served as a Special Assistant United States Attorney at the United States Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District of New York. Ms. Szczepanik received her JD from Georgetown University and her BS in Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania.
